info.title: Marz API
info.description:
Backend de Marz. Fuente de verdad del contrato HTTP entre marz-api y marz-front.
Se consume con `oapi-codegen` (server) y `openapi-typescript` + `openapi-fetch` (client).

get.description: Read-only email operational metrics for Grafana. Requires the BACKOFFICE_METRICS_TOKEN service bearer token; Clerk user JWTs are not accepted.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Exclusive range end. Maximum range is 93 days.

parameters.name: X-Brand-Workspace-Id
parameters.description:
Active brand workspace ID. Required for brand accounts (returns 422
brand_workspace_required if missing or malformed). Ignored for creator
accounts but clients should always send it for consistency.

get.description:
Returns the current state of a brief-builder processing token. Used by
the front to recover after a page reload during the wizard. Token TTL
is 30min while pending/in_progress and 5min after completion.

500.description: Internal Server Error. See §4.1.8.

InboxInlineActionActor.description: Actor that may render and execute this inline action. Valid values are `brand` and `creator`.

Message.description: JSON-encoded SES event payload delivered by SNS.

BrandWorkspaceSummary.description:
Active brand workspace for the authenticated account. Present only when
kind=brand and onboarding is complete. The frontend uses `id` to populate
the X-Brand-Workspace-Id header on every request.
MVP assumes 1 brand account = 1 workspace; once multi-workspace lands,
a dedicated /v1/brand-workspaces endpoint will replace this embedding.

tier.description: Tier declarado por el creator en paso C4

size_bytes.description: Exact size in bytes; the presigned URL is signed with this Content-Length.

required_headers.description: Headers que el cliente debe incluir en el PUT a S3

max_bytes.description: Tamaño máximo del archivo en bytes

pdf_s3_key.description: S3 key of the uploaded PDF, when a pdf was provided. Echo back as `brief_pdf_s3_key` in `POST /v1/campaigns`.

BriefProcessingStateResponse.description:
State of a brief-builder processing token. `brief_draft`,
`fields_filled_count` and `fields_empty_count` are populated only when
`state` is `completed` or `partial`; null/zero otherwise.

BriefDraft.description:
AI-generated brief content. Same shape used in the WS
`campaigns.brief.processing.completed` event under `brief_draft.brief`.

CampaignBonusAmount.description:
Bonus amount discriminated by `type`. When `type` is `percentage`, the
`percentage` field is required and must be between 1 and 100. When
`type` is `fixed`, `amount` and `currency` are required; `currency`
must match the campaign's `budget_currency`.

percentage.description: Required when type is percentage. Ignored otherwise.

amount.description: Decimal string strictly greater than 0. Required when type is fixed.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code matching the campaign's budget_currency. Required when type is fixed.

active_id.description: Stable per-campaign item key. Equal to the creator account id because a campaign can have only one active participant row per creator.

participant_id.description: Stable per-campaign item key. Equal to the creator account id because a campaign can have only one participant row per creator.

event_type.description: Domain event identifier (e.g. offer.sent, draft.submitted, stage.opened).

payload.description: Self-contained snapshot of the event at emission time. Shape depends on event_type.

currency.description: Ignored when sent; currency is inherited from the campaign.

event_type.description: Identifier of the event. Acts as the discriminator across all WS event types.

schema_version.description: Optional payload schema version. Defaults to "v1" when absent.

payload.description: Event-specific payload. Shape determined by event_type.

WSEventMessageCreated.description:
Emitted on the WS channel when a chat message is created. The payload is
discriminated by `type` and is shape-compatible with TextMessageListItem /
SystemEventMessageListItem from GET /v1/conversations/{id}/messages, plus
`conversation_id` and `client_message_id` (text only).

client_message_id.description:
Echo of the client_message_id supplied on POST /v1/conversations/{id}/messages.
Used by the client to reconcile optimistic inserts with the server-confirmed
message in the timeline.

author_account_id.description: Zero UUID — system events have no human author.

text.description: Human-readable label of the event (e.g. "Offer accepted").

event_type.description: Domain event identifier (e.g. offer.accepted, stage.opened, draft.submitted).

payload.description: Self-contained snapshot of the domain event at emission time. Shape depends on event_type.

WSCampaignEventEnvelope.description:
Emitted to subscribers of `campaign:{campaign_id}` after campaign read-model
handlers materialize Discovery, Offer, Deliverable, or Payment lifecycle events.

event_id.description: Source domain event id used for client-side de-duping.

reason.description: Source lifecycle event type, e.g. OfferAccepted or DraftApproved.

source_ref_type.description: Source event/ref type used in the activity uniqueness key.
